People being sad that others wouldn't get to experience it

If we go bankrupt I'll fund the game by making mukbang live streams

me I’m the negative one

-# I loved the collab but now I’m just salty that it felt like it cost Ryoshu canto in some pacing issues due to no intervallo for her before canto, she got one after but imo by that point the damage had been done
Literally PM will not bankrupt unless they upsize and screw up their budget(according to KJH)
But would a second Intervallo would really change it? They seemed to have always wanted to throw a curveball at us with Canto 9 starting with a raid
Ryoshu kinda had ttt but yea no this is a fair point, I do feel like the ring intervallo helped a lot tho
Considering they write Intervallo on the fly, that would've change how Canto 9 started

Yeah, that is one good point and I can't argue with it, but it's not a be all end all since they still have more story and time to explore Ryo. It's not like canto 9 was pure unfettered ass(at least not to me). 

Even if I do understand the people who complain, I am very glad the Collab happened since more eyes and players means more money for better game.
And it did very much fit the limbus story, have my favorite boss fight gameplay up to that point and have some of the best music put out by EIN

content: u know this guy even want solo logos

> ||Viy, real name Stjopa Razin 
> He's at least 30 years old
> Leader of the independent Ursus tactical unit "Sechin" and a representative of the so-called "Free People," marginalized group descended from non-Ursus races who were once promised autonomy but instead were abandoned, displaced, and used as expendable labor and soldiers.
> He was born to a family with diluted Nightzmora bloodline. That's why he's pink, he's run out of ink.
> His childhood was shaped by hardship: his father was disabled, his mother blind, his sister was gone(?), and his village was ultimately destroyed by a catastrophe. 
> Later, he was taken by an Ursus noble and forced into the military system in exchange for his parents' safety.
> Viy undergoing extreme training and becoming a "War Caster," where among hundreds of children, only the luckiest would earn the title.
> He served as a spy, assassin, and commander in unrecorded border wars. 
> Over time, he realized many of these conflicts were meaningless internal power struggles, where civilians were sacrificed and soldiers treated as disposable tools.
> He eventually defected, faked his death, and returned to his ruined village. There, he began rebuilding his life and formed Sechin.
> Paperwork is his biggest weakness. He can read and write just fine, but he really dislikes it, so reports and documentation slow him down a lot. Despite of that, he spends a surprising amount of time in the library.
> He has a habit of quietly solving problems for others, never taking credit, never being seen, but making sure things got better.
> In EP17, he solos three War Casters at once, hijacks a Fourth Army's warship, then goes full throttle to smash his boss' frigate, jumped off the deck and somehow landed safely.
> He wanted to spar with Logos.||
